Did You Know? Windows' Shutdown Isn't Actually Shutting Down
Newer Windows versions ship with 'Fast Startup' on by default — Shutdown actually dumps system state to a file and reloads it next boot. Why, and how to turn it off.
Update Your Project Packages with npm-check-updates
Updating packages on an old project is painful. Here's how to use npm-check-updates to scan your package.json, see what's upgradable, and bump versions in one shot.
Another Way to Declare Variables in Vue 3 Composition API
Was chatting with a friend about Vue's Composition API and flipped through the docs again — spotted an interesting section worth a quick post.
Tired of Windows 10 Forcing Updates on You? Here's How to Disable Auto-Updates

Windows 10's forced update behavior is the worst — slowing your machine mid-work, throwing random new bugs, or worst of all bluescreen on a botched update. This post walks through disabling the Windows Update service from the Services UI. Heads up — this also disables security updates, so only do it if you understand the security trade-off.
A Fun Little Game — Gandalf Adventures Prompt Injection Walkthrough

For people designing AI tools, the Prompt is really important — it determines what the AI says back. There happens to be a little game called "Gandalf Adventures" that's all about Prompt Injection, so I figured I'd also write up a walkthrough.
GitHub Pages Lets You Set Up Your Own 404 Page Too

GitHub Pages actually supports a custom 404 page out of the box — no extra config needed. This post explains what a 404 page is for (UX and SEO), what GitHub Pages' default 404 looks like, and how to drop a 404.html (or 404.md with permalink) into your repo root to ship your own.
Sooner or Later You'll Hit This: What's the Difference Between ref and reactive in Vue 3 Composition API?

In Vue 3's Composition API you can create reactive data with either ref or reactive — but what's actually different between the two? This post digs into the differences with some examples and a peek at the source code.
Hexschool Hotel API (freyja): Full Backend Deployment Guide
A backend deployment guide for the "Hexschool Hotel API (freyja)" project from Hexschool.
"Localhost Refused to Connect" on Taiwan's HiCOS Citizen Certificate? Here's the Fix

If you're trying to use Taiwan's Citizen Digital Certificate on a government site and your browser keeps showing "localhost refused to connect" — usually the problem is ChkSrv.exe not running in the background. This post walks through how to fix it.
A Casual Chat About TypeScript — What It Is and Why Use It

TypeScript has basically become a must-have skill for a lot of developers, but plenty of projects are still on JavaScript and on the fence about migrating. So this post is a casual share of my own take on TypeScript and why you'd want to use it.
